sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · API DESIGN
sheet.title: Chapter 6 – API Design & Communication Flow
sheet.p: The Theta Chat Bot follows a RESTful API architecture, where the chatbot widget communicates with the FastAPI backend through secure HTTPS endpoints.

The backend acts as the central orchestration layer responsible for validating requests, retrieving relevant knowledge, communicating with the Large Language Model (LLM), and returning structured responses to the frontend.
All communication between components is performed using JSON payloads, making the APIs lightweight, language-independent, and easy to integrate with existing or future frontend applications.
The communication between system components follows the sequence below:
Each component has a clearly defined responsibility, ensuring loose coupling and easier maintenance.
The following endpoints are proposed for the initial version of the chatbot.

jfield.val: Process user queries and generate chatbot responses
Create or resume a user session
Submit user feedback on chatbot responses
Health check for monitoring and deployment validation
Retrieve application version information
Retrieve chatbot configuration and feature flags (Optional)

sheet.p: Future administrative endpoints may include analytics, knowledge synchronization, and dashboard APIs.

When a visitor submits a message, the frontend sends a POST request to the chatbot API endpoint. The request body uses a structured JSON schema to pass the user message and session context to the backend.
sheet.callout: Note: The request schema shown below is indicative. Field names, optional parameters, and request structure may be adjusted during implementation to fit final integration requirements.

json-block.label: SCHEMA DEFINITION
json-block.note: Sample payload only. Field values are illustrative and will be populated at runtime.

jfield.val: String | Required | Unique identifier for the current browser session.
String | Required | The user's natural language question or input text.
ISO 8601 | Optional | Client-side timestamp of the request.

json-block.label: SAMPLE REQUEST PAYLOAD
json-block.note: Sample payload only. Field values are illustrative and will be populated at runtime.

jfield.val: What AI services does Theta Technolabs provide?

sheet.p: Upon receiving the request, the backend performs the following operations:

steps.step: Retrieve the Top-K relevant content chunks.
Send the prompt to the configured LLM.
Return the final response to the frontend.

sheet.p: Each step is executed sequentially to ensure consistent and reliable responses.

The backend returns a standardized JSON response envelope. Every successful response includes a status indicator, the AI-generated answer, source references used to construct the response, a confidence classification, and backend processing metadata.
sheet.callout: Note: The response schema shown below is a representative sample. The exact field names, confidence scoring levels, and source reference structure may be refined during implementation and adjusted at runtime without breaking the core API contract.

json-block.label: SCHEMA DEFINITION
json-block.note: Sample payload only. Field values are illustrative and will be populated at runtime.

jfield.val: String | Required | Execution result: success or error.
String | Required | Echo of the session identifier from the request.
String | Required | The AI-generated natural language answer.
Array | Optional | List of knowledge base pages used.
String | Required | Confidence classification: High, Medium, or Low.
Integer | Required | Total end-to-end processing time.

json-block.label: SAMPLE SUCCESS RESPONSE
json-block.note: Sample payload only. Field values are illustrative and will be populated at runtime.

jfield.val: Theta Technolabs provides AI Development, Machine Learning...
[ { "title": "AI Development Services", "url": "https://thetatechnolabs.in/services/ai" } ]

sheet.p: Source references are included to increase transparency and allow users to verify the information directly on the Theta Technolabs website.

The API returns a standardized error envelope whenever a request cannot be completed successfully. All error responses share the same top-level structure as success responses to simplify client-side parsing.
sheet.callout: Note: Error codes and messages are indicative. Additional error codes may be introduced at runtime as new edge cases are identified during testing and production operation.

json-block.label: SCHEMA DEFINITION
json-block.note: Sample payload only. Field values are illustrative and will be populated at runtime.

jfield.val: String | Always error for failed requests.
String | Machine-readable error identifier.
String | Human-readable description of the error.

json-block.label: SAMPLE ERROR RESPONSE
json-block.note: Sample payload only. Field values are illustrative and will be populated at runtime.

jfield.val: Relevant information could not be found within the current knowledge base.
Invalid or incomplete request payload
Session is no longer valid
No relevant knowledge found
Language model request timed out
API rate limit exceeded
Unexpected server error

sheet.p: Providing standardized error codes simplifies debugging and future monitoring.

jfield.val: OK | Request processed successfully
Bad Request | Invalid request
Unauthorized | Authentication required (Future)
Not Found | Endpoint or resource not found
Too Many Requests | Rate limit exceeded
Internal Server Error | Unexpected backend error
Service Unavailable | LLM or database temporarily unavailable

sheet.p: Although the chatbot is publicly accessible and does not require user authentication, temporary browser sessions are maintained to support conversational context.
Each visitor receives a unique session identifier when opening the chatbot.
The session identifier enables:
No personally identifiable information (PII) is required to create or maintain a session.
To protect the chatbot APIs from misuse, the backend implements multiple validation and security mechanisms.
These include:

list.item: Secure server-side communication with LLM providers

sheet.p: Sensitive API credentials are never exposed to the frontend.
All public endpoints are versioned using URI-based versioning.
Example: /api/v1/chat
Versioning allows future API enhancements without breaking compatibility with existing frontend integrations.

jfield.val: Simple, widely supported, and easy to integrate.
Lightweight, language-independent communication.
Improves conversational continuity without user auth.
Increases transparency and user trust.
Simplifies debugging and monitoring.
Supports future enhancements with backward compatibility.
